content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO 25804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441          322-6444

March 13, 1979

Betty Boone
Chief Deputy
Office of the San Diego
County Counsel
355 County Administration Center
1600 Pacific Highway
San Diego, CA 92101

Dear Betty:

     This will confirm today's telephone conversation.

     You informed me that your husband is a sole practitioner.
As part of his activities, he defends criminal cases by
appointment for the County of San Diego ("County"). He is
paid by the County. You asked whether you were required to
disclose on your Statement of Economic Interests your com-
munity property share of the payments he receives from the
County.

     I advised you that the payments from the County were
excluded from the definition of income under Government Code
Section 82030(b)(2). Although Government Code Section 82030(b)(2)
excludes "salary" from a state, local or federal government
agency, the staff has interpreted salary to include payments
such as those your husband received from the County for his
criminal defense work. Therefore, you do not have to disclose
your community property share of his County payments on your
Statement of Economic Interests.

     If you have any other questions or comments, please
call.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   [illegible]
                                   Barbara Campbell
                                   Counsel
                                   Legal Division

BC:kp

sections.question: You asked whether you were required to disclose on your Statement of Economic Interests your community property share of the payments he receives from the County.
sections.conclusion: The payments from the County were excluded from the definition of income under Government Code Section 82030(b)(2). Although Government Code Section 82030(b)(2) excludes "salary" from a state, local or federal government agency, the staff has interpreted salary to include payments such as those your husband received from the County for his criminal defense work. Therefore, you do not have to disclose your community property share of his County payments on your Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a person must disclose on their Statement of Economic Interests their community property share of their spouse's income from county-appointed criminal defense work.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Payments from the County for criminal defense work are excluded from the definition of income under Government Code Section 82030(b)(2), so the spouse does not need to disclose their community property share of such payments on the Statement of Economic Interests.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Straightforward advice letter with clear Q&A structure embedded in narrative form. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present, but question and conclusion content are clearly identifiable. Addressee is Betty Boone, Chief Deputy, San Diego County Counsel.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked whether you were required to disclose on your Statement of Economic Interests your community property share of the payments he receives from the County.

CONCLUSION: The payments from the County were excluded from the definition of income under Government Code Section 82030(b)(2). Although Government Code Section 82030(b)(2) excludes "salary" from a state, local or federal government agency, the staff has interpreted salary to include payments such as those your husband received from the County for his criminal defense work. Therefore, you do not have to disclose your community property share of his County payments on your Statement of Economic Interests.
embedding.first_500_words: March 13, 1979 Betty Boone Chief Deputy Office of the San Diego County Counsel 355 County Administration Center 1600 Pacific Highway San Diego, CA 92101 Dear Betty: This will confirm today's telephone conversation. You informed me that your husband is a sole practitioner. As part of his activities, he defends criminal cases by appointment for the County of San Diego ("County"). He is paid by the County. You asked whether you were required to disclose on your Statement of Economic Interests your community property share of the payments he receives from the County. I advised you that the payments from the County were excluded from the definition of income under Government Code Section 82030(b)(2). Although Government Code Section 82030(b)(2) excludes "salary" from a state, local or federal government agency, the staff has interpreted salary to include payments such as those your husband received from the County for his criminal defense work. Therefore, you do not have to disclose your community property share of his County payments on your Statement of Economic Interests. If you have any other questions or comments, please call. Sincerely, Barbara Campbell Counsel Legal Division
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that a Chief Deputy County Counsel is not required to disclose on her Statement of Economic Interests her community property share of her husband's income from county-appointed criminal defense work, as such government payments are excluded under Government Code Section 82030(b)(2).
